mechanism: Multi‑target TKI with activity against BCR‑ABL and reported FLT3 inhibition
additional_notes: Potent TKI; activity against FLT3 reported in some studies. 10‑point, 3‑fold dilution.

mechanism: Reversible inhibition of the 26S proteasome causing proteotoxic stress and apoptosis
additional_notes: Tested in MOLM‑13; proteasome inhibition induces apoptosis in AML. 10‑point, 3‑fold dilution.
Epigenetic modulator with activity in AML cells. 10‑point, 3‑fold dilution.

mechanism: Inhibits BET bromodomains (reduces MYC and transcriptional programs)
additional_notes: Also known as Birabresib; BET inhibition decreases proliferation in AML models. 10‑point, 3‑fold dilution.

mechanism: Inhibits HSP90, leading to degradation/destabilization of client oncoproteins (e.g., FLT3)
additional_notes: Also called Tanespimycin; destabilizes FLT3 and other oncogenic kinases in FLT3‑ITD AML. 10‑point, 3‑fold dilution.

mechanism: Selective MEK1/2 inhibition targeting the MAPK pathway
additional_notes: Targets MAPK pathway; sometimes synergizes with FLT3 inhibitors. 10‑point, 3‑fold dilution.

mechanism: Multiple reported effects (STAT3/Wnt inhibition, mitochondrial uncoupling/respiratory effects)
additional_notes: Repurposing candidate with anti‑leukemic activity in screens; 10‑point, 3‑fold dilution.

mechanism: ALDH inhibition and induction of proteotoxic stress (anticancer activity often Cu‑dependent)
additional_notes: Often requires copper cofactor for anticancer activity; recommended co‑treatment with ~1 µM CuCl2 (fixed) during testing. 10‑point, 3‑fold dilution.

mechanism: Disrupts microtubules and other proposed anticancer mechanisms
additional_notes: Anthelmintic with reported AML cytotoxicity. 10‑point, 3‑fold dilution.

mechanism: Multiple off‑target effects (Hedgehog pathway, angiogenesis, mTOR signaling)
additional_notes: Repurposing candidate with in vitro anticancer activity. 10‑point, 3‑fold dilution.

mechanism: Inhibits mitochondrial complex I and alters cellular metabolism
additional_notes: Anti‑proliferative effects often require high concentrations in vitro; be mindful of osmolarity/vehicle. 10‑point, 3‑fold dilution.

mechanism: Induces oxidative stress and apoptosis; effective in APL and cytotoxic in other AML at µM levels
additional_notes: Effective in APL; has cytotoxic activity in non‑APL AML at micromolar concentrations. 10‑point, 3‑fold dilution.

mechanism: Inhibits HMG‑CoA reductase, altering cholesterol/isoprenoid synthesis and impacting proliferation/survival pathways
additional_notes: Statins show anti‑proliferative effects in some AML models. 10‑point, 3‑fold dilution.

mechanism: Induces ROS and apoptosis; reported activity against leukemia stem cells in some studies
additional_notes: Leprosy drug with reported pro‑apoptotic/ROS effects in leukemia; 10‑point, 3‑fold dilution.
